sections.label: Section 1364
sections.original: ἀρκεῖν δοκεῖ μοι · τοὺς γὰρ ἐν μέσῳ λόγους
sections.translation: That is enough , I think . As for the recounting of intervening events ,

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: πολλαὶ κυκλοῦνται νύκτες ἡμέραι τʼ ἴσαι ,
sections.translation: many are the circling nights and an equal number of days which will reveal them to you clearly , Electra .

sections.label: Section 1367
sections.original: σφῷν δʼ ἐννέπω γε τοῖν παρεστώτοιν ὅτι
sections.translation: And this is my advice to you two , since you stand there : now is your opportunity to act , now Clytaemnestra is alone , now no man is inside . But if you pause ,

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: φροντίζεθʼ ὡς τούτοις τε καὶ σοφωτέροις
sections.translation: consider that you will have to fight both those inside and others mightier and better skilled .

sections.label: Section 1372
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν μακρῶν ἔθʼ ἡμὶν οὐδὲν ἂν λόγων ,
sections.translation: Pylades , in no way does our task call any longer for many words , but rather demands that we enter the house immediately , after first adoring the shrines of my father ’ s

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.translation: gods , the keepers of these gates . Orestes and Pylades enter the house , followed by the Paedagogus . Electra remains outside .

sections.label: Section 1376
sections.translation: King Apollo ! Hear them with favor , and hear me besides , who so often have come before your altar with hands rich in such gifts as I could obtain ! And now , O Lycean Apollo , with what means I have

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.translation: I pray to you , I supplicate you , I implore you , be our ready champion in these designs , and show what rewards the gods bestow on humans in return for their impiety ! Exit Electra , into the house .

sections.label: Section 1.1384
sections.translation: Behold how Ares stalks onward ,

sections.label: Section 1.1385
sections.translation: breathing bloody vengeance that is hard to oppose . Just now have the hunters of wicked crimes passed beneath that roof there , the hounds which none may flee . And so not long shall

sections.label: Section 1.1390
sections.translation: the vision of my soul hang in suspense .

sections.label: Section 1.1391
sections.translation: The champion of the spirits infernal is ushered on guileful feet into the house , the rich , ancestral palace of his father , and he bears keen-edged death in his hands .

sections.label: Section 1.1395
sections.translation: Maia ’ s son Hermes , who has shrouded the guile in darkness , leads him right to his goal and delays no longer .

sections.label: Section 2.1398
sections.translation: My dearest friends , in a moment the men will do the deed . But wait in silence .

sections.label: Section 2.1400
sections.translation: How do they fare ? What are they doing now ?

sections.label: Section 2.1401
sections.original: λέβητα κοσμεῖ , τὼ δʼ ἐφέστατον πέλας .
sections.translation: She is decking the urn for burial ; the two of them stand close to her .

sections.label: Section 2.1402
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐκτὸς ᾖξας πρὸς τί ;
sections.translation: And why have you hurried out ?

sections.label: Section 2.1402a
sections.translation: To guard against Aegisthus entering before we are aware .

sections.label: Section 2.1404
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh ! Our house

sections.label: Section 2.1405
sections.original: φίλων ἔρημοι , τῶν δʼ ἀπολλύντων πλέαι .
sections.translation: is empty of friends and filled with murderers !

sections.label: Section 2.1406
sections.original: βοᾷ τις ἔνδον · οὐκ ἀκούετʼ , ὦ φίλαι ;
sections.translation: Someone shouts inside . Do you not hear , friends ?

sections.label: Section 2.1407
sections.translation: I heard , ah , me , sounds unfit to be heard , and I shudder !

sections.label: Section 2.1408
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινʼ · Αἴγισθε , ποῦ ποτʼ ὢν κυρεῖς ;
sections.translation: Ah , misery ! Aegisthus , where , where are you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1409
sections.translation: Look , once more someone cries out !

sections.label: Section 2.1410
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον τέκνον , οἴκτιρε τὴν τεκοῦσαν .
sections.translation: My son , my son , have pity on your mother !

sections.label: Section 2.1413
sections.original: ὦ πόλις , ὦ γενεὰ τάλαινα , νῦν σοι
sections.translation: Wretched city , wretched race , now the fate that has held you day by day perishes — it perishes !

sections.label: Section 2.1415
sections.translation: Oh , I am wounded !

sections.label: Section 2.1416
sections.translation: Ah , wounded again !

sections.label: Section 2.1417
sections.translation: Would that Aegisthus , too , were wounded !

sections.label: Section 2.1418
sections.translation: The curses bring fulfillment : those who are buried live .

sections.label: Section 2.1420
sections.translation: For men long dead are draining their killers ’ blood in a stream of requital .

sections.label: Section 2.1422
sections.original: καὶ μὴν πάρεισιν οἵδε · φοινία δὲ χεὶρ
sections.translation: And now they are here ! The red hand drips with sacrifice to Ares , and I cannot blame the deed .

sections.label: Section 2.1424
sections.translation: Orestes , what happened ?

sections.label: Section 2.1424a
sections.translation: All is well within the house ,

sections.label: Section 2.1425
sections.translation: if Apollo ’ s oracle spoke well .

sections.label: Section 2.1426
sections.translation: The miserable woman is dead ?

sections.label: Section 2.1427
sections.translation: Have no more fear that your haughty mother will ever again trample on your rights .

sections.label: Section 2.1429
sections.original: παύσασθε · λεύσσω γὰρ Αἴγισθον ἐκ προδήλου .
sections.translation: Quiet ! For I see Aegisthus in plain sight .

sections.label: Section 2.1430
sections.translation: You , young men , get back inside !

sections.label: Section 2.1431
sections.translation: He is at our mercy walking from the suburb , full of joy .

sections.label: Section 2.1433
sections.translation: Go with all speed to the vestibule , so that , just as your first task prospered , so this one again may prosper now .

sections.label: Section 2.1435
sections.original: νῦν , τὰ πρὶν εὖ θέμενοι , τάδʼ ὡς πάλιν .
sections.translation: Have courage . We will accomplish it .

sections.label: Section 2.1436
sections.translation: Hurry , then , to wherever you wish .

sections.label: Section 2.1437
sections.translation: See , I am gone .

sections.label: Section 2.1438
sections.translation: Things here will be my concern . Exeunt Orestes and Pylades , into the house .

sections.label: Section 2.1439
sections.original: διʼ ὠτὸς ἂν παῦρά γʼ ὡς ἠπίως ἐννέπειν
sections.translation: It would be well to whisper into this man ’ s ear some few words of seeming gentleness ,

sections.label: Section 2.1440
sections.original: πρὸς ἄνδρα τόνδε συμφέροι , λαθραῖον ὡς
sections.translation: so that he may rush blindly upon his trial before Justice .

sections.label: Section 1442
sections.original: τίς οἶδεν ὑμῶν ποῦ ποθʼ οἱ Φωκῆς ξένοι ,
sections.translation: Which of you can tell me where those Phocian strangers are , who are said to have brought report for us that Orestes passed away amidst the shipwrecked chariots ?

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: σέ τοι , σὲ κρίνω , ναὶ σέ , τὴν ἐν τῷ πάρος
sections.translation: You , you I ask , yes , you , who were in former days so bold . It seems to me that this concerns you most , so you must know best , and can best tell me .

sections.label: Section 1448
sections.original: ἔξοιδα · πῶς γὰρ οὐχί ; συμφορᾶς γὰρ ἂν
sections.translation: I do know . How could I not ? Otherwise I would be an alien to the fortune of my nearest kinsmen .

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: ποῦ δῆτʼ ἂν εἶεν οἱ ξένοι ; δίδασκέ με .
sections.translation: Where , then , may the strangers be ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 1451
sections.translation: Inside . They have found a way to the heart of their hostess .

sections.label: Section 1452
sections.original: ἦ καὶ θανόντʼ ἤγγειλαν ὡς ἐτητύμως ;
sections.translation: Have they in fact reported him truly dead ?

sections.label: Section 1453
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ κἀπέδειξαν , οὐ λόγῳ μόνον .
sections.translation: No , not reported only . They have shown him .

sections.label: Section 1454
sections.original: πάρεστʼ ἄρʼ ἡμῖν ὥστε κἀμφανῆ μαθεῖν ;
sections.translation: Then I can identify the corpse myself ?

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.original: πάρεστι δῆτα , καὶ μάλʼ ἄζηλος θέα .
sections.translation: You can , indeed , though it is no enviable sight .

sections.label: Section 1456
sections.original: ἦ πολλὰ χαίρειν μʼ εἶπας οὐκ εἰωθότως .
sections.translation: You have indeed given me a joyful greeting , beyond your custom .

sections.label: Section 1457
sections.original: χαίροις ἄν , εἴ σοι χαρτὰ τυγχάνοι τάδε .
sections.translation: May joy be yours , if joy is what you find in these things .

sections.label: Section 1458
sections.translation: Silence , I say , and throw wide the gates for all Mycenaeans and Argives to see ,

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: ὡς εἴ τις αὐτῶν ἐλπίσιν κεναῖς πάρος
sections.translation: so that , if any one of them were once buoyed by empty hopes in this man , now by seeing his corpse , he may welcome my bit in his mouth , instead of waiting until my punishment makes him grow wits by force !
